quotes.text: The cold doesn't bother me half as much as the quiet.
quotes.speaker: Fargo (1996)
quotes.context: Marge Gunderson making small talk with Norm on a winter morning
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Marge's plainspoken Midwestern warmth and her comfort with mundane observation are spot-on for her character. The giveaway is that Marge usually directs her observations outward at the world; musing on personal feeling is slightly out of register for her.

quotes.text: You fly casual, or you don't fly at all.
quotes.speaker: Star Wars: Return of the Jedi (1983)
quotes.context: Han Solo briefing the team before the Endor mission
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — 'fly casual' is actually a real-adjacent Han Solo phrase from this era, which is exactly what makes it dangerous for players. The giveaway is the rhythm; Han's real quips tend to be reactive rather than instructional.

quotes.speaker: The Princess Bride (1987)
quotes.context: Westley the farmhand says this to Buttercup; it's revealed to be his way of saying "I love you," and recurs throughout the film.

quotes.speaker: Shrek (2001)
quotes.context: Shrek explains to Donkey that ogres have layers

quotes.text: Every man in that trench was somebody's son. Don't you ever let yourself forget that.
quotes.speaker: Saving Private Ryan (1998)
quotes.context: Captain Miller speaking quietly to Sergeant Horvath after a firefight
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Hanks's Miller carries this kind of moral exhaustion throughout the film and speaks in plain, soldierly sentences. The giveaway is a faint sentimentality; Miller more often deflects personal feeling than names it this plainly.

quotes.text: You keep calling it a negotiation, Frank. It stopped being a negotiation when you walked in without a lawyer.
quotes.speaker: Heat (1995)
quotes.context: Lt. Hanna pressing a suspect during an interrogation
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — the clipped, procedural tension fits Mann's style perfectly, and Pacino's Hanna is exactly the type to reframe the room's power dynamic mid-scene. The subtle giveaway is that Hanna rarely names suspects so directly; his confrontations tend to be more elliptical.
